unitTitle: Unit 1: Present simple, continuous & stative verbs

content.subtitle: Examples
items: Tom **catches** the bus to school every morning.
My aunt **lives** in a small town by the sea.

content.subtitle: Useful notes
content.description: These time expressions are commonly found with the present simple:
Frequency adverbs normally come before the main verb, but they come after the verb **be**.
items: I **usually walk** to school in the morning.
She is **always** the first to arrive.

content.subtitle: Be careful
notes: The verbs **be** and **have** have irregular present forms.

rows.label: negative
rows.right: He/she/it **is not (isn't / 's not) playing** ... You/we/they **are not (aren't / 're not) playing** ...

content.subtitle: Use
items: Things happening at this very moment
Situations that are temporary and will change

content.subtitle: Examples
items: Sam **is talking** on the phone right now.
Lucy **is staying** with her grandparents this summer.
My sister **is always leaving** her clothes on the floor!

content.subtitle: Useful notes
content.description: These time expressions signal that the present continuous is being used:
**Always** with the present continuous expresses annoyance at something someone keeps doing.
items: Dad **is always forgetting** where he puts his glasses!

content.description: Stative verbs express states — feelings, opinions, senses — rather than actions. Because they describe a condition rather than something happening, we do not normally use them in continuous forms.

content.subtitle: Useful notes
content.description: Stative verbs belong to categories describing inner or permanent conditions, so simple tenses are used.
items: Emotions and preferences: **love, hate, prefer** — She **prefers** tea to coffee.
Mental processes: **know, believe, understand** — He **believes** every word you say.
Ownership: **have, belong to** — This jacket **belongs to** my cousin.
Senses and appearance: **seem, appear** — The idea **seems** a bit unusual.

content.subtitle: Be careful
notes: A small number of stative verbs — including **be**, **have** and **think** — can appear in continuous tenses when they refer to an action rather than a state.
What do you **think** of her new haircut? (= opinion — stative)
**I'm thinking** of changing jobs. (= actively considering — action)

sections.title: A: Present simple from picture prompts
sections.instructions: Look at the prompts and write sentences. Use the correct present simple form.

items.sentence: ...... (every morning / walk to school)
items.answer: Every morning, Jake walks to school.

items.sentence: ...... (usually / drink tea for breakfast)
items.answer: She usually drinks tea for breakfast.

items.sentence: ...... (after school / often / do her homework in the library)
items.answer: After school, she often does her homework in the library.

items.sentence: ...... (twice a month / visit her grandparents)
items.answer: She visits her grandparents twice a month.

items.sentence: ...... (never / stay up late on weekdays)
items.answer: She never stays up late on weekdays.

items.sentence: ...... (play basketball / every Saturday)

sections.title: B: Present continuous
sections.instructions: Complete using the correct present continuous form of the verbs in brackets. You may have to use some negative forms.

items.sentence: Be quiet! The baby ...... (sleep).
We ...... (not / watch) the film because it's boring.
At the moment, Carla ...... (study) for her science test.
Tom ...... (stay) with his cousins this week.
I ...... (not / joke)! I'm serious!
My brother ...... (always / borrow) my headphones without asking!
We ...... (eat) dinner right now. Can I call you later?
...... (you / use) the computer at the moment?

sections.title: C: Rewrite correctly
sections.instructions: Rewrite correctly. Change the words or phrases in bold.

items.sentence: **Are students needing** a lot of sleep during exams?
items.answer: Do students need a lot of sleep during exams?

items.sentence: Why are you so quiet? I hope you **don't feel** upset.
items.answer: Why are you so quiet? I hope you aren't feeling upset.

items.sentence: My dad **is working** in a bank in the city centre.
items.answer: My dad works in a bank in the city centre.

items.sentence: **Does Anna read** that magazine right now?
items.answer: Is Anna reading that magazine right now?

items.sentence: I **am always taking** the bus to college.
items.answer: I always take the bus to college.

items.sentence: This game console **is working** with two batteries.
items.answer: This game console works with two batteries.

items.sentence: Look at the score! **Does our team play** well today?
items.answer: Look at the score! Is our team playing well today?

items.sentence: Most teenagers **are liking** music festivals.

sections.title: D: Circle the correct word or phrase
sections.instructions: Circle the correct word or phrase.

items.sentence: I **stay / am staying** with my uncle this month.
We **don't eat / aren't eating** meat very often.
Olivia **does / is doing** her homework right now.
**Does Ben usually drive / Is Ben usually driving** to work?
In tennis, you **hit / are hitting** the ball over the net.
I **check / am checking** my emails every evening.
**Do they study / Are they studying** for the exam at the moment?
Maria and Ellie **become / are becoming** more confident these days.
A good teacher **understands / is understanding** their students.
How **do you pronounce / are you pronouncing** this word?

sections.title: E: Complete with present simple or present continuous
sections.instructions: Complete using the correct present simple or present continuous form of the verbs in the box. You may have to use some negative forms.

items.sentence: Tourists usually ...... in this hotel during the summer.
...... you ...... to the radio, or can I switch it off?
Regular exercise ...... you feel healthier.
I ...... my sister's coat today because mine is dirty.
...... Emma always ...... the shopping on Fridays?
...... you ...... for your keys? They're on the table.
You ...... that box correctly. It's going to fall.
This backpack ...... to my brother.

sections.title: F: Correct the wrong tenses
sections.instructions: Underline ten verbs in the wrong tense and rewrite them correctly.
sections.passage: 'One hobby I **am enjoying** (1) ...... a lot is painting. First, you **are choosing** (2) ...... the colours you want to use. It **is looking** (3) ...... easy at first, but good painting takes practice. You **are needing** (4) ...... patience and creativity. When people make mistakes, they sometimes **are throwing** (5) ...... their work away and starting again. You **are improving** (6) ...... by practising regularly. Some people **are preferring** (7) ...... photography, but I **am not believing** (8) ...... it's more interesting than painting. Right now, I **paint** (9) ...... a picture for my bedroom. My sister **practises** (10) ...... the guitar in the next room, so it's quite noisy!'
